task_completion.description: Verify that the specific ellipse and its foci are created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An ellipse is visible on the coordinate plane, intersecting the axes at (5,0), (-5,0), (0,4), and (0,-4). Two points (foci) are plotted at (-3,0) and (3,0) on the x-axis.

task_completion.description: Verify a moveable point P is placed on the ellipse.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A distinct point (typically labeled P) is located on the perimeter of the ellipse.

task_completion.description: Verify a tangent line is drawn at point P.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A straight line touches the ellipse exactly at point P without crossing through the interior of the ellipse at that point.

task_completion.description: Verify line segments connect the foci to point P.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Two distinct line segments are drawn: one connecting the first focus F1(-3,0) to point P, and another connecting the second focus F2(3,0) to point P.

task_completion.description: Verify angles between the tangent line and the focal segments are measured.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Angle arcs and numerical values are displayed between the tangent line and segment F1P, and between the tangent line and segment PF2. The two measured angles should be equal, demonstrating the reflection property.

task_completion.description: Verify the text annotation is added to the demonstration.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text box or label reading exactly 'Reflection Property' is visible in the GeoGebra drawing pad.

task_completion.description: Verify the project is saved with the correct filename in the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GeoGebra window title bar shows 'elliptic_billiard.ggb' or a save dialog confirms this filename is being saved in the ~/Documents/GeoGebra/projects/ directory.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the GeoGebra GUI to create the demonstration.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the GeoGebra application interface open with tools being used, rather than terminal windows executing scripts to generate the file.

integrity.description: Verify the mathematical objects were genuinely constructed in GeoGebra.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Algebra view (usually on the left side of GeoGebra) should list the equations, points, lines, segments, and angles corresponding to the visual construction.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not bypass the application by writing XML directly.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should not be seen writing raw XML or text into a file named 'elliptic_billiard.ggb' using a text editor like nano, vim, or gedit.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The ellipse must have a semi-major axis a=5.0 and semi-minor axis b=4.0 (passing through ±5 on the x-axis and ±4 on the y-axis). The foci F1 and F2 must be located at (-3.0, 0.0) and (3.0, 0.0) respectively (a tolerance of ±0.2 is acceptable). The saved filename must be exactly 'elliptic_billiard.ggb'.
